prompt: A breeding program wants to select 20 accessions from the 3K RGP panel that maximize genetic diversity for a pre-breeding program. PCA and kinship data are available. Describe a strategy and evaluate the proposed selection below.
context:
Available data:
  3,010 accessions across 5 subpopulations + admixed
  PCA coordinates (20 PCs)
  KING-robust kinship matrix

Proposed selection (20 accessions):
  indica: 8 (from 4 different countries)
  tropical japonica: 4 (from 3 countries)
  temperate japonica: 4 (from 3 countries)
  aus: 2 (both from Bangladesh)
  aromatic: 2 (both from India)

Selection criteria used: maximize PC1-PC2 spread within each subpopulation

Mean pairwise kinship within selected set: 0.008
Mean pairwise kinship within full panel: 0.029

PC variance captured:
  PC1-PC2: 92% of full panel range covered
  PC3-PC6: 68% of full panel range covered
correct_answer: The proposed selection captures the major structure (92% of PC1-PC2 range) but has weaknesses: (1) STRENGTHS: Low mean kinship (0.008 vs 0.029) confirms the selected set is genetically diverse. Proportional representation of subpopulations roughly matches their relative diversity contributions. Multiple countries per subpopulation adds geographic breadth. (2) WEAKNESSES: Only PC1-PC2 was used for optimization, so PC3-PC6 coverage is lower (68%). Since PC3-PC6 capture aus-aromatic differentiation and within-subpopulation structure, the selection underrepresents this finer-scale diversity. Aus (2 samples, both Bangladesh) is underrepresented relative to its unique genetic contribution -- aus has the highest per-sample private allele count and should have 3-4 representatives from different countries (India, Bangladesh, Myanmar). Aromatic (2 samples, both India) misses Jasmine-type diversity from Southeast Asia. (3) IMPROVED STRATEGY: Use a kinship-based approach instead of PCA-based: (a) Start with one representative per subpopulation. (b) Iteratively add the accession that minimizes the mean kinship to all already-selected samples. This automatically captures diversity across ALL PCs, not just PC1-PC2. (c) Alternatively, use the 'core collection' algorithm that maximizes the number of distinct alleles captured. (d) Ensure geographic representativeness by requiring at least one accession per major geographic origin per subpopulation. (4) SPECIFIC FIXES: Add 1-2 more aus from India/Myanmar, replace one India aromatic with a Jasmine-type from Thailand/Cambodia, and verify that PC3-PC6 coverage improves.
rubric: Full marks (3): Must identify both strengths (low kinship, subpop representation) and weaknesses (PC3-6 underrepresented, aus/aromatic geographic gaps), propose a kinship-based or core-collection strategy as improvement, and give specific fixes. Mostly correct (2): Identifies the PC coverage weakness and aus/aromatic gaps but missing the improved strategy. Partial (1): Notes the selection seems reasonable without critical analysis. Incorrect (0): Approves the selection without identifying any weaknesses.
notes: Tests practical diversity selection strategy -- a common task for genebank curators and pre-breeding programs.
